script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'speed-quality-investigation',
  description: 'Serious investigation: where does deep-run wall-time actually go, why does the 16-way compose deadlock at scale, and what speedups keep deep coverage + faithfulness. Ranked, quality-safe plan.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Profile', detail: '4 parallel lenses: retrieval, compose-deadlock, whole-pipeline, quality-constraints' },
    { title: 'Stress-test', detail: 'adversarially verify each proposed speedup keeps coverage + faithfulness' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'Fable: ranked safe speedup plan', model: 'fable' },
  ],
}

const CTX = `
POLARIS SPEED-VS-QUALITY INVESTIGATION. Established facts (measured today):
- A deep report render (route_all = 328 of 329 baskets) takes ~30+ min END-TO-END, and the AGENTIC
  RETRIEVAL phase (the react loop fetching URLs one turn at a time) dominates (~24 min); the COMPOSE
  phase is the smaller slice.
- We ported compose_fix's 16-way concurrency into outline_agent. The OFF-LOOP to_thread wrap is
  verdict-safe. But the aggressive PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS + semaphore(48) DEADLOCKED at full
  328-basket scale: 19/20 threads stuck in futex_wait, 0 progress 8.8min, had to SIGKILL. The small
  verdict-identity A/B passed but never exercised full-scale concurrency.
- Also observed: the outline can DEGRADE to seed+partial under load (must stay cp4_used=agentic).

QUALITY INVARIANTS ANY SPEEDUP MUST KEEP (non-negotiable):
1. DEEP COVERAGE: render ~328/329 baskets (route_all). Do NOT speed up by dropping baskets/coverage.
2. FAITHFULNESS: strict_verify gate intact; kept/dropped NLI verdict set unchanged; no unverified/
   derived number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx]. Speed must be verdict-identical.
3. AGENTIC: cp4_used=agentic, never degrade-to-seed as a shortcut.
4. NO DEADLOCK: a speedup that can hang is not a speedup.

READ-ONLY: read code in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent and /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix; run cheap
deterministic profiling; write findings ONLY to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.
Do NOT edit either worktree (a wheel is live in outline_agent). Source key for any measured run:
set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a. Report REAL measured numbers; if you can't measure, say so.
`

const FIND = {
  type: 'object', required: ['lane', 'findings'],
  properties: {
    lane: { type: 'string' },
    findings: { type: 'array', items: {
      type: 'object', required: ['what', 'evidence', 'proposed_speedup', 'quality_risk'],
      properties: {
        what: { type: 'string', description: 'the bottleneck/mechanism at file:line or measured' },
        evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'REAL number or code cite, not a guess' },
        proposed_speedup: { type: 'string', description: 'the concrete change and its expected time saving' },
        est_saving: { type: 'string', description: 'rough wall-time saved' },
        quality_risk: { type: 'string', description: 'which of the 4 invariants it could threaten, and how to keep it safe' },
      } } },
  },
}
const VERDICT = {
  type: 'object', required: ['keeps_quality', 'reason'],
  properties: {
    keeps_quality: { type: 'boolean', description: 'does this speedup keep coverage+faithfulness+agentic+no-deadlock' },
    real_saving: { type: 'string' },
    reason: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

const LANES = [
  { key: 'retrieval', prompt: `LANE: RETRIEVAL — the dominant ~24-min cost. Read the agentic outline's react/retrieval loop (search_more_evidence, fetch_url, live_retriever, the react turn loop in outline_agent.py). Determine WHY it's slow: sequential per-turn URL fetches? no fetch concurrency? redundant re-fetches? per-turn LLM decide latency? too many turns for 328 baskets? Measure fetch count / turn count if you can. Propose speedups that KEEP evidence quality: parallel/batched fetching, a fetch cache, capping turns without losing coverage, prefetch. For each, state the quality risk (must not drop coverage or fabricate).` },
  { key: 'deadlock', prompt: `LANE: COMPOSE DEADLOCK ROOT-CAUSE. The 16-way port deadlocked at 328-basket scale (19/20 threads futex_wait). Read the ported code in outline_agent multi_section_generator.py (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS basket-workers + off-loop to_thread) and compose_fix's original. Find the ACTUAL lock: is a shared semaphore (judge slot / LLM semaphore / get_semaphore) acquired ACROSS the off-loop thread boundary, or re-entrantly, causing a thread-pool ⨯ semaphore deadlock? Why did compose_fix gate parallel-verify OFF by default? Give the safe subset that CANNOT deadlock (likely off-loop-only, or basket-workers with a non-shared/timeout-guarded semaphore) and the exact fix.` },
  { key: 'pipeline', prompt: `LANE: WHOLE-PIPELINE PROFILE. Break the end-to-end deep run into phases (retrieval, outline build, credibility pass, compose draft, strict_verify, dedup/consolidate, RACE score) and estimate each phase's wall-time share from the real logs (outputs/_16way_run.log, step*_compose.log). Which phase is the biggest lever AFTER retrieval? Is the 41% sentence-drop rate causing wasted compute (drafting sentences that get dropped)? Rank the phases by time and by speedup-leverage.` },
  { key: 'quality-guard', prompt: `LANE: QUALITY-CONSTRAINT AUDIT. For EACH speedup idea likely to come up (parallel retrieval, fetch cache, turn cap, off-loop-only compose, basket-worker sharding, lower verify concurrency, batched judge), enumerate exactly which of the 4 invariants (deep coverage / faithfulness verdict-identity / cp4_used=agentic / no-deadlock) it threatens and the concrete guard that keeps it safe. Flag any 'speedup' that only works by quietly dropping baskets, weakening the verify gate, or degrading to seed — those are DISQUALIFIED.` },
]

phase('Profile')
const perLane = await pipeline(
  LANES,
  lane => agent(`${CTX}\n\n=== YOUR LANE ===\n${lane.prompt}`, { label: `probe:${lane.key}`, phase: 'Profile', schema: FIND }),
  (res, lane) => {
    if (!res || !(res.findings || []).length) return []
    return parallel(res.findings.map(f => () =>
      agent(`${CTX}\n\nADVERSARIALLY VERIFY this proposed speedup. Does it REALLY save meaningful wall-time AND keep ALL 4 quality invariants (deep coverage, faithfulness verdict-identity, cp4_used=agentic, no-deadlock)? Default to keeps_quality=FALSE if it risks any invariant or the saving is marginal.\n\nSPEEDUP: ${f.proposed_speedup}\nCLAIMED SAVING: ${f.est_saving}\nSTATED RISK: ${f.quality_risk}\nEVIDENCE: ${f.evidence}`,
        { label: `verify:${lane.key}`, phase: 'Stress-test', schema: VERDICT })
        .then(v => ({ ...f, lane: lane.key, verdict: v }))))
  },
)
const survivors = perLane.flat().filter(Boolean).filter(f => f.verdict && f.verdict.keeps_quality)

phase('Synthesize')
const plan = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nYOU ARE FABLE. Synthesize the RANKED, QUALITY-SAFE speedup plan from the speedups that
survived adversarial quality-verification:\n${JSON.stringify(survivors, null, 2)}\n
Deliver: (1) the end-to-end time breakdown and the single biggest honest lever. (2) A RANKED plan —
what to do first for the most wall-time saved while keeping ALL 4 invariants, with the guard for each.
(3) The compose-deadlock: the exact safe fix (and confirm the off-loop-only fallback is deadlock-free
+ verdict-identical). (4) An honest ceiling: after the safe speedups, roughly how fast can a deep
328-basket faithful run get, and what CANNOT be sped up without sacrificing quality. Do not propose
anything that drops coverage, weakens faithfulness, or degrades to seed — those are off the table.`,
  { label: 'fable:speedup-plan', phase: 'Synthesize', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' },
)

log(`SPEED INVESTIGATION: ${survivors.length} quality-safe speedups survived`)
return { survivors, plan }

survivors.what: Within-search URL fetching is already parallel — so intra-search fetch concurrency is NOT the bottleneck (rules out one hypothesis).
survivors.evidence: live_retriever.py:6363-6441: parallel_fetch(max_workers=band 8-14 scaled by candidate count, per_task_timeout=120s, per-host cap 4). Measured per-search fetch 12.7-56.3s is bounded by the slowest URL in a ~8-23 URL batch, not sequential per-URL fetching.
survivors.proposed_speedup: No change here; do NOT invest in intra-search fetch concurrency. Focus effort on R1/R2 (turn-level parallelism + exhaustion breaker), which target the real ~53% non-fetch overhead and ~24% zero-yield waste.
survivors.quality_risk: N/A — this is a negative finding that redirects effort away from a dead end; raising max_workers further would add provider load with no measured benefit.

verdict.real_saving: ~0 runtime wall-time. This is a negative/anti-finding, not a runtime speedup: it correctly recommends NOT building intra-search fetch concurrency (which already exists) and redirecting effort to R1/R2. The only 'saving' is avoided wasted engineering effort plus avoided added provider load / futex-contention risk.
verdict.reason: Evidence verified against live code and the measured log. live_retriever.py:6363-6441 confirms intra-search URL fetch is ALREADY parallel: max_workers band 8-14 (constants _FETCH_WORKERS_FLOOR=8 / _CEILING=14 at lines 149/156, per_candidate=16), per-host cap 4, per_task_timeout=120s, host-keyed semaphore so distinct hosts fetch concurrently. Per-search wall is bounded by the slowest URL in the batch, not sequential fetching. The measured breakdown corroborates the cited waste: loop 737s, fetch 346s -> 391s non-fetch = 53%; zero-yield stretch 179s/737s = 24%. So fetch-layer concurrency is provably NOT the bottleneck; the real waste is turn serialization + redundant re-fetch, which R1/R2 target. keeps_quality=TRUE because the finding prescribes no code change: coverage, faithfulness verdict-identity, cp4_used=agentic, and no-deadlock are all trivially preserved, and it further AVOIDS the deadlock/provider-load risk that raising max_workers would introduce. The finding is sound; the only honest correction is that its 'saving' is effort-avoidance, not measurable wall-time.

result.plan:
POLARIS SPEED-VS-QUALITY — FINAL SYNTHESIS (written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md, "FINAL SYNTHESIS" section)

Context that shapes everything below: after adversarial verification, exactly ONE finding survived, and it is a negative one — intra-search URL fetching is already parallel (live_retriever.py:6363-6441, workers band 8-14, per-host cap 4), so fetch concurrency is a dead end. Every affirmative speedup was disqualified on invariant or marginal-saving grounds. The honest plan is "protect the floor, fix the tail, defer one small safe lever."

(1) END-TO-END BREAKDOWN + BIGGEST HONEST LEVER (measured, logs/step4_route_all_compose.log, 329 baskets, completed clean, 1449.7s = 24.2 min):
- Seed outline digest 142s (9.8%) | AGENTIC REACT LOOP 737s (50.8%) = fetch 346.3s + non-fetch LLM ~391s (24 decides ≈ 170s + ~11 checklists); 179s (24%) of the loop was zero-yield re-fetch churn | dedup/consolidate 33s (2.3%) | COMPOSE + strict_verify 514s (35.5%) | render 27s (1.9%).
- The brief's "30+ min / ~24-min retrieval" is the BAD-TAIL case (_16way_run: one 466s mega-fetch of 162/200 URLs, then the 900s wall tripped → degrade-to-seed).
- Single biggest honest lever: TAIL CONTROL — the ~6-10 min gap between the clean 24.2-min run and the 30+-min bad-tail run. It is also an I3 quality bug (the tail run degraded to seed). No legal mechanism dents the clean-run 737s loop.

(2) RANKED PLAN (all 4 invariants kept, guard per item):
- P0 — Pin the safe compose config: shipped off-loop asyncio.to_thread wrap (multi_section_generator.py:6079/:6130) + PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (confirmed current default; no .env/launch override) + judge cap in 4-8 band + PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. GUARD: startup assert refusing workers>1 or sem≥48 until a re-engineered path passes a full-328 verdict-identity A/B. Saving vs live baseline: 0; vs the 16-way attempt: hang → completion.
- P1 — Fix the bad tail as a correctness bug (~6-10 min on affected runs + restores I3). No cleared mechanism yet; constraint: no fetch-cap/turn cuts; raising the outline wall is quality-preserving even though slower.
- P2 — 100%-overlap per-aspect exhaustion skip (only salvageable affirmative lever; DEFERRED to next wheel — needs the read-only live_retriever seam). Skip fetch iff every candidate URL (after the same url-dedup rule) is already in ev_store → kept=0 guaranteed → ev_store byte-identical → verdict-identical by construction; ledger transitions unchanged. Measured ceiling ~1-2 min (turns 8-11 fetch = 74s + classify overhead), not the earlier 2-3 min claim. GATE: full-328 A/B + cp4_used==agentic + ~328/329 rendered.
- P3 — Nothing else; do-not-repropose list recorded (R1 batch drain, R3 cheaper decide, turn caps, basket sharding + judge-cap clamp, judge-cap moves, batched judge, bytes cache, section concurrency 3→8, prompt/regen changes — each with its disqualifying invariant).

(3) COMPOSE DEADLOCK — EXACT SAFE FIX: root cause is multiplicative thread oversubscription with no escape valve (sections 3 × workers 16 × ~2 inner-TPE amplification contending on one global BoundedSemaphore; binding entailment acquire at entailment_judge.py:155 has NO timeout; map join and to_thread have no wall; `with TPE` shutdown(wait=True) re-hangs on a wedged worker). The fix that ships is P0: off-loop + workers=1, guard-enforced. Explicitly do NOT add a timeout to the binding acquire (JudgeSlotTimeout → fail-closed ('ENTAILED','judge_error') → dropped sentence → I2 violation) and do NOT ship the judge-cap clamp (collapses to serial at safe knobs; concurrency needs J≈48 which re-opens the 429 storm). Off-loop-only fallback CONFIRMED deadlock-free + verdict-identical: workers=1 → no nested pool, serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE; peak judge demand 3 ≤ cap 4; 150s POST deadline; empirically, the full 329-basket run completed clean on this exact config (1449.7s, no SIGKILL, faithfulness_pass=true, leaked_cite_ev_tokens=0, agentic held — finish_outline bounced at turn 18 and kept looping).

(4) HONEST CEILING: a faithful, agentic, full-coverage deep render is ~24 min TODAY when nothing goes wrong — that is near the floor. With P2 (next wheel, A/B-gated): ~22-23 min clean; P1 collapses the 30+-min tail toward the clean number. Realistic floor ~20-22 min on current models. Cannot be sped up without sacrificing quality: the serial decide/checklist chain (~391s — it IS the agentic convergence engine; changing it changes the evidence pool → verdict drift), cross-turn fetch overlap (blocked by the decide dependency; intra-search fetch already parallel), the strict_verify/NLI gate at unstarved token budgets under the 4-8 judge cap, drop-driven repair/regen (regen is the coverage-rescue path), and the 329-basket compose+verify work itself — that work is the product, not overhead.

summary: Serious investigation: where does deep-run wall-time actually go, why does the 16-way compose deadlock at scale, and what speedups keep deep coverage + faithfulness. Ranked, quality-safe plan.
